title: Cottage Bench with Storage Cubbies
summary: An entryway bench with a slatted back, 2x2 legs, and two open cubbies underneath the seat — sized for a basket on each side.
description:
This is a straightforward pocket-hole build with one genuinely nice design touch: the slatted back is easy to reconfigure. The plan calls for a fixed number of evenly-spaced 1x2 slats, but an X-back, fewer wider slats, or more narrow ones all work as long as the overall height and width stay the same — so treat the back as the one place to make this bench your own without changing anything structural.

The optional arched top on the back rail is a nice detail for very little extra work: mark in from each end and down slightly, cut the curve with a jigsaw, then round it over with a sander until it reads as an arch rather than a jigsaw cut. Build the storage box with its pocket holes facing down and inward before assembly — once the front and back frames go on, you won't be able to see or reach them.

tools.note: Only needed for the optional arched-top back detail.

materials.name: Pine, 1x12, 10 ft
materials.note: Seat, sides, center divider, cubby bottom.

cutList.note: Width matches your actual 1x12 — adjust to fit.

steps.title: Build the slatted back
steps.body: Mark the back 1x3 boards for slat placement and drill a 3/4" pocket hole in each end of every slat. With glue applied, one 1-1/4" pocket hole screw per end is enough. For the optional arched top, measure in 9" from each end of the top back board and down 1", draw a line between the marks, cut with a jigsaw, and round the arch over with a sander.

steps.title: Attach the back to the legs
steps.body: Attach the back panel to the back legs with two 3/4" pocket holes per end and 1-1/4" pocket hole screws. Attach the bottom 2x2 the same way.

steps.title: Build the storage box
steps.body: Set your pocket-hole jig to the 3/4" setting. Drill four pocket holes along both long edges of the seat and cubby bottom for the front and back frames (step 6) — keep these facing down so they're hidden once finished. Drill two pocket holes on each end of the bottom piece and on the top ends of the side pieces. Attach the sides to the seat, the bottom to the sides, then position the center divider and attach it with brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Trim the storage box ends
steps.body: Measure and cut the 1x2 end trim pieces to fit, then attach with 1-1/4" brad nails and glue.

steps.title: Build the leg assembly
steps.body: Set the pocket-hole jig to the 1-1/2" setting and join the 2x2 front legs and rails with 2-1/2" pocket hole screws and glue. Nail and glue the 1x2 front trim to the top.

steps.title: Join the front and back assemblies to the storage box
steps.body: Attach the front leg assembly and the back panel assembly to the middle storage section through the predrilled pocket holes. Add extra screws anywhere it feels like it needs more hold.

images.alt: An entryway bench with a slatted back and two open storage cubbies underneath the seat
